summary: Iteration 6: verified async-job add-site flow, new /sftp/test endpoint, and frontend Test/Add buttons. Updated /app/backend/tests/test_add_site_multi.py to the new {job_id}+poll contract and added 3 new tests for /sftp/test (happy path, missing creds, RBAC) plus add-status RBAC. Full suite: 55/55 passing (was 51/51). Frontend end-to-end: Test connection returned green result (16 items), Add site showed live '…' polling messages then final ✗ 'no .html pages…' — self-cleaned. Regular admin correctly does NOT see the add-site form. State restored: sites=[wifetobe, demo-couk]; wifetobe SFTP remote_path reset to /home/u897891218/domains/wifetobe.org/public_html; add_jobs collection cleared.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/sites/add now returns {job_id, slug} promptly (<3s observed) and enqueues _run_add_job as an asyncio task tracked in _bg_tasks — correct fix for the reverse-proxy timeout issue.

Job state persisted in Mongo add_jobs (survives worker restart) — good. State field values 'starting'|'pulling'|'ingesting'|'done'|'error' cover the pipeline. Consider TTL index on add_jobs.created (e.g., 24h) to auto-prune, otherwise long-running instances accumulate rows.
Self-clean on failure works: after rebex 'no HTML' error, /app/sites/<slug> was removed and db.sites row deleted; add_jobs row remains (deliberate, for polling) but no orphan site data.
POST /sftp/test (require_super, slug-less) returns ok:true count=16 for rebex — verified. Missing-creds returns ok:false gracefully.

Frontend polling interval 2s and shows the message from the job unchanged — good UX. Missing-fields hint 'Fill in: Short ID, Host, Username, Password' precisely tracks missing inputs. Both buttons disabled correctly until minimum inputs.
Regular admin UI correctly hides the add-site form (gated on user.role === 'superadmin') while still showing the 'Sites on this app' list — matches spec.
Nit: axios call in addSite() uses timeout:30000 but backend returns in <1s now; consider 10s. Not a bug.
Nit: interval-based polling in App.js addSite() has no upper bound / cap — if the job hangs 'pulling…' forever, UI polls indefinitely. Consider a max-attempts guard (e.g., 60 iterations = 2 min).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_add_site_multi.py (rewrote test_add_site_real_pull_no_html_selfcleans to poll add-status; added test_add_status_rbac, test_sftp_test_endpoint, test_sftp_test_missing_creds, test_sftp_test_rbac)
test_credentials: admin@wifetobe.org / ChangeMe!2026 (role=superadmin). Rebex public SFTP test.rebex.net:22 demo/password /pub/example reachable from sandbox and returns 16 items with no HTML (expected).
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral test users created + deleted per test (test_admin_*, test_editor_*, test_rbac_*, test_sftp_*, and one UI-flow admin ui_admin@test.com). One UI-flow slug 'uijob' auto-self-cleaned. add_jobs collection cleared after run. wifetobe SFTP remote_path restored.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_add_site_multi.py must remain a single class (xdist loadscope). The add-site job poll cap is 30s in tests — rebex pull takes ~15s. If you need to test a SUCCESSFUL HTML pull, upload an index.html to a controllable SFTP first; Hostinger is unreachable from sandbox. Two new endpoints exercised: POST /api/sftp/test (super-only, slug-less body: SftpSettings) and GET /api/sites/add-status/{job_id} (super-only). Contract: /api/sites/add returns {job_id, slug} — do NOT expect the old {ok, ingested} shape.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures. The main-agent fix (async background job + Mongo-persisted state + 2s frontend polling) verified end-to-end against real SFTP (test.rebex.net).
